narrative_blocks.header: Married a New Haven hardware merchant at twenty-three
narrative_blocks.body: Daughter of Joseph Darling and Amelia Mills Darling of Connecticut, she married James Dwight III on 13 March 1811 at New Haven at age twenty-three. He was twenty-six, fresh from a Yale education and starting his hardware business with his older brother.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at twenty-five leaving infant Elizabeth
narrative_blocks.body: She bore James one daughter Elizabeth Smith Dwight in 1812 and died in 1813 at twenty-five. James remarried two years later to Susan Breed in 1815; the infant Elizabeth was raised by her stepmother and grew to marry into the Bradley family before her own early death at thirty-six in 1848.

narrative_blocks.header: Married a New Haven hardware merchant at twenty-three
narrative_blocks.body: Daughter of Joseph Darling and Amelia Mills Darling of Connecticut, she married James Dwight III on 13 March 1811 at New Haven at age twenty-three. He was twenty-six, fresh from a Yale education and starting his hardware business with his older brother.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at twenty-five leaving infant Elizabeth
narrative_blocks.body: She bore James one daughter Elizabeth Smith Dwight in 1812 and died in 1813 at twenty-five. James remarried two years later to Susan Breed in 1815; the infant Elizabeth was raised by her stepmother and grew to marry into the Bradley family before her own early death at thirty-six in 1848.

burial.plot_notes: Grove Street Cemetery, New Haven CT — with father Pope of Yale and daughter Aurelia

narrative_blocks.header: Twin brother John died at nineteen, 1803
narrative_blocks.body: Born 1 September 1784 at Greenfield Hill CT, he was the twin of John Dwight who died at age 19 in 1803 — leaving him the only surviving male of that birth-day pair among Pope Dwight's children. The twin's death came when James was just nineteen and starting his hardware apprenticeship.

narrative_blocks.header: Hardware merchant from New Haven to Petersburg
narrative_blocks.body: He went into hardware business with his older brother Timothy Dwight Jr at New Haven, then carried the trade to Petersburg VA. He returned to New York and established the hardware firm James & George A. Dwight before moving south again later in life to Georgia.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost his first wife at twenty-five, 1813
narrative_blocks.body: On 13 March 1811 at New Haven he married Aurelia Darling, daughter of Joseph and Amelia Mills Darling. She bore him one daughter Elizabeth Smith Dwight in 1812 and died in 1813 at age 25 — leaving him a widower with an infant. He remarried 8 August 1815 at Norwich CT to Susan Breed.

After decades of hardware trade between Connecticut, Virginia, New York, and Georgia, he returned to New Haven in 1854 at age 70 and retired there. He died 24 March 1863 at 78 — outliving by 60 years his twin brother John who had died at 19. Father of Tim V the 12th Yale president by Susan Breed.

career.location: New Haven CT → Petersburg VA → New York → Georgia
career.notes: Hardware partnership with brother Timothy Dwight Jr starting at New Haven; carried trade to Petersburg VA; established James & George A. Dwight hardware firm in NYC; later returned south to Georgia. Retired to New Haven 1854.

narrative_blocks.header: Motherless at one, half-sister to four Dwights
narrative_blocks.body: Her mother Aurelia Darling died in 1813 when she was one year old. Her father James Dwight III remarried Susan Breed in 1815 and had five more children; Elizabeth was raised with the half-Dwight siblings until her own death at thirty-six in 1848.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost her mother at one, raised by stepmother
narrative_blocks.body: Born 1812 in New Haven CT to James Dwight III and his first wife Aurelia Darling, she lost her mother at age one when Aurelia died in 1813. Raised by her stepmother Susan Breed Dwight alongside her five half-siblings, she died at thirty-six in 1848 — half a generation before her father's 1863 death.
